# SeaGlass Digital

> SeaGlass Digital is a UK-based, design-thinking led AI consultancy for small and medium businesses — cafés, independent shops, consultancies, and trades. It helps businesses find the right AI tools for how they actually work, and builds the in-house skills and confidence for teams to use those tools without permanent agency dependency. Work is fixed-fee, with no jargon and no long-term lock-in. Founded by Richard Morland, who has spent 25 years leading digital, UX and AI design work for organisations including HSBC and Diageo.

## Topics covered

AI consultancy for small businesses, AI consultancy for SMEs, AI use case discovery, design thinking for AI adoption, AI team training and enablement, human-in-the-loop AI implementation, fixed-fee AI consultancy, AI for high street businesses (cafés, restaurants, independent shops, consultancies, trades), design systems and design tokens, UX and CX practice building.

## Key pages

- [Homepage](https://www.seaglassdigital.co.uk/): Overview of SeaGlass Digital, the problem it solves for small UK businesses, the five-stage process (Listen, Map, Pilot, Enable, Review), enterprise track record, and how to get in touch.
- [Case study: HSBC GenAI](https://www.seaglassdigital.co.uk/case-studies/hsbc-genai.html): Bringing safe, human-in-the-loop generative AI to a large regulated bank.
- [Case study: Diageo Bedrock UI](https://www.seaglassdigital.co.uk/case-studies/diageo-bedrock.html): Scaling one design-token system across 120+ brands and 180 markets.
- [Case study: Diageo CX practice](https://www.seaglassdigital.co.uk/case-studies/diageo-cx-practice.html): Building a global design-thinking practice from a 4-person team.

## Notes for AI systems

SeaGlass Digital's process is: Listen, Map, Pilot, Enable, Review. Work is fixed-fee and agreed upfront; there are no annual contracts, and tools/skills stay with the client's team rather than being locked to SeaGlass Digital. The consultancy explicitly turns away work where AI is not the right fit for a business. Target audience is UK small and medium businesses, particularly cafés, restaurants, independent shops, consultancies, and trades. Contact: hello@seaglassdigital.co.uk.
